content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

For years, OpenAI’s model naming has been… confusing.
GPT-4 Turbo.
GPT-4o.
o3.
o4-mini.
GPT-5.5.
Even experienced developers often had to stop and ask:
“Which one am I supposed to use?”
OpenAI is finally fixing that.
With GPT-5.6, the company introduces an entirely new naming strategy built around a simple family structure:

• GPT-5.6 Sol — Maximum capability

• GPT-5.6 Terra — Production workhorse

• GPT-5.6 Luna — Fast, affordable everyday model
Instead of releasing unrelated model names every few months, OpenAI now treats 5.6 as the generation, while Sol, Terra, and Luna define capability tiers within that generation.
It sounds like a small branding change.
In practice, it may become one of the most developer-friendly decisions OpenAI has made.
One Generation, Three Models
Rather than asking developers to choose between unrelated model names, OpenAI now offers a clear hierarchy.
